behavior: Mostly ground-foraging and diurnal; typically runs into cover when disturbed and flies to trees to roost.
field_marks: Adult male with an iridescent cobalt-blue head, neck, and breast
Upright fan-shaped crest of slender shafts tipped blue-green
White stripe above the eye and white crescent below it
Scaled bronze-green upperparts and black-and-white barred wing coverts
Very long green-bronze upper-tail covert train with prominent eyespots in adult males
Strong pale gray-brown legs; male commonly bears a spur
habitat: Open deciduous woodland, forest edge, scrub, grassland, cultivated land, and other terrestrial areas with cover, water, and suitable roost trees.
